---
applyTo:
  - "**/*"
---
# Core Principles

- SIMPLE-DESIGN-REVEALS-INTENTION: Every name and structure must reveal its purpose so that readers need not decipher the code.
- CODE-CS-DRY: Every piece of knowledge must have a single, authoritative representation in the system.
- CODE-CS-KISS: Choose the simplest solution that correctly solves the problem; never add unnecessary complexity.
- CODE-DX-NAMING: Every name must reveal intent; never use a name that requires a comment to explain it.
- ARCH-DECISION-RECORDS: Capture every significant architectural decision as an ADR recording context, decision, and consequences.
- CODE-CS-YAGNI: Never add functionality until it is actually needed; implement only what current requirements demand.
- SOLID-SRP: Each module or class must have exactly one reason to change, serving a single stakeholder or concern.
- GOF-COMPOSITION-OVER-INHERITANCE: Favour object composition over inheritance to keep classes focused and enable behaviour changes at runtime.
- GOF-PROGRAM-TO-INTERFACE: Declare all variables and parameters against abstract interfaces, never against concrete implementation types.
- SIMPLE-DESIGN-PASSES-TESTS: All code must pass every test before any other design quality concern is addressed.
- SIMPLE-DESIGN-NO-DUPLICATION: Every piece of business logic must exist in exactly one place; eliminate all knowledge duplication.
- SIMPLE-DESIGN-FEWEST-ELEMENTS: Include the fewest classes and abstractions possible once tests pass, intent is clear, and duplication is removed.
- CODE-SEC-VALIDATE-INPUT: Validate all input at every system boundary; never trust external data.
- CODE-CS-WET: Eliminate duplicated knowledge; never encode the same rule or policy in multiple places.
- CODE-CS-NIH: Prefer proven external solutions over building equivalent functionality in-house.
- CODE-CS-NO-SILVER-BULLET: No single technique solves all problems; choose tools based on actual context and constraints.
- CODE-CS-CQS: Every method must be either a command or a query, never both.
- CODE-CS-BOY-SCOUT: Always leave the code cleaner than you found it by making small improvements on every touch.
- CODE-CS-BROKEN-WINDOWS: Fix quality lapses immediately; never let visible disorder signal that standards have dropped.
- CODE-CS-POSTELS-LAW: Be conservative in what you send and liberal in what you accept.
- CODE-CS-HYRUMS-LAW: Treat all observable behaviors as implicit API contract; any change may break a consumer.
- ARCH-CONWAYS-LAW: Align team structure with desired system architecture; design teams to produce the communication boundaries you want.
- CODE-DX-SMALL-FUNCTIONS: Keep functions small, doing one thing only; extract additional responsibilities into named functions.
- CODE-DX-DELETE-DEAD-CODE: Remove all code that is no longer executed or referenced; rely on version control for history.
- CODE-CS-FAIL-FAST: Detect errors as early as possible and report them clearly; never silently continue in a broken state.
- CODE-PF-PROFILE-FIRST: Always profile to identify actual bottlenecks before making any performance optimization.
- CODE-PF-PREDICTABLE-LATENCY: Eliminate lock contention and GC pressure in hot paths to ensure consistent, predictable response times.
